description_long: This is a four-story apartment building with a flat, gravel-textured roof and a symmetrical facade. The exterior is clad in reddish-brown horizontal siding, punctuated by evenly spaced white-framed windows, most of which have window-mounted air conditioning units. The front of the building features a recessed entryway with a small awning above the main door, flanked by two ground-level windows. The windows are arranged in a grid-like pattern, contributing to the building's orderly appearance. There are no balconies or decorative architectural elements, and the structure is boxy and utilitarian. The building is typical of mid-20th-century urban residential architecture found in dense city neighborhoods.
description: A four-story, reddish-brown apartment building with a flat roof, white-framed windows, and a recessed main entrance. The facade is symmetrical and features window-mounted air conditioners.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with an awning, ground-level windows, and a symmetrical arrangement of windows with air conditioners.
description_view_1: This side view displays the building's flat roof and the regular grid of windows, with no visible entrance or decorative features.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the side, showing a uniform window arrangement and flat roof, with no entrance or distinguishing features.
description_view_3: The opposite side view is similar to view 1, with evenly spaced windows and a plain facade.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a symmetrical, evenly spaced grid pattern across all floors, with each floor having a consistent number and alignment of windows.
